memory.summary: My plan to finish the analysis is suddenly interrupted by a text from cohort_3 inviting us for lunch. Since I'm already sleep-deprived, this social pull feels urgent and hard to ignore.
The lunch invite from cohort_3 feels urgent, but my 2-hour sleep debt and analysis deadline make me hesitate. I'll decline to protect my focus.
They're gathering now, but I'm too drained to join. Declining protects my analysis time; social pressure won't override my deadline.
Lunch is cancelled; cohort_3 understands my deadline. I'll double down on the analysis now.
My energy is critically low and sleep debt is mounting, yet the analysis deadline looms large. I must prioritize my work now, ignoring social pressure to finish strong.
Energy is critically low with mounting sleep debt, yet the analysis deadline forces me to ignore social pressure and double down on work now.

desires: Complete the reading analysis by 20:00 without any further social interruptions, even if it means ignoring my physical exhaustion.
Secure a perfect grade on this assignment to validate my discipline over their expectations that I should have been sleeping.
Maintain an unshakeable reputation among cohort_3 as someone who prioritizes academic rigor above all else, proving that my 'declines' were strategic choices, not personal rejections.

goals.description: Complete the reading analysis by 20:00, strictly enforcing a digital silence zone to eliminate all social friction and protect my focus capacity.
goals.rationale: The deadline is imminent, and every second spent negotiating with cohort_3 or teammate_A is a second stolen from my academic output. My impulsive drive screams for distraction, but my assertive value system demands I cut it off now. If I let them interrupt me, I lose the day's hard-earned focus capacity, and that grade is the only metric that validates my discipline against their expectations.

goals.description: Submit the assignment by 20:00 without looking incompetent, treating this as a binary transaction where output equals competence regardless of my physical exhaustion.
goals.rationale: I've already burned mental energy fighting off social leakage. This task is purely transactional—output equals competence—but I'm not going to start until the high-priority boundary is secured. No more multitasking while tired; I need to lock in the grade first before worrying about the fatigue.

agent.last_reasoning: Deadline is 20:00; I'm at 20:15 and only 95% done. My assertive value demands I finish this task now to secure the grade, ignoring social friction. Any delay risks failing the metric that validates my discipline.

goals.description: Grab lunch with cohort_3 immediately and eat without guilt.
goals.rationale: Ignoring them now feels like a personal failure; social connection is the only thing keeping me from spiraling right now, even if it delays reading.

goals.description: Finish the analysis section of the assignment before 18:00.
goals.rationale: I can't focus on this until I've had food. The deadline is real, but doing it while stressed will just make the output lazy anyway.
Low priority compared to the analysis deadline. If I skip it, so be it; the guilt of skipping is worse than the penalty for missing it.

desires: Finish the analysis section before 18:00 so I don't have to explain why my progress looks lazy to the professor.
Get out of this room and eat lunch with cohort_3 immediately; ignoring them now feels like a personal failure, even though I'm already two hours behind on sleep.
Not feel guilty about skipping the reading later if it means I can grab that last bite of food without looking stressed.

action.name: propose_boundary
action.reasoning: Eating lunch now is the only way to maintain focus for the analysis deadline. I'll eat with them but set a hard 45-min limit so I don't guilt-trip myself into skipping study time entirely.

goals.description: Finish the analysis section of the assignment by 18:00 without social interruptions.

goals.description: Set a hard stop for work at 19:30 to secure sleep despite feeling exhausted.
goals.rationale: Sleep discipline is low in my baseline, but I know that total exhaustion tomorrow will tank my focus capacity for the next day's coursework. It's a necessary trade-off: sacrificing some leisure tonight to ensure I can actually perform academically tomorrow. No guilt allowed on this one.
